matches.x: CHAPTER XVII. Jehoshaphat prospereth—He sendeth Levites with the princes to teach Judah—His enemies terrified by God—His greatness. A™ Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his: stead, and strengthened himself against Israel.

And in the case of Jehoshaphat also, when he was installed in the kingship, he did not occupy himself with the business of kingship nor with honor but with the business of judging.14Note that Jehoshaphat’s name means, “The LORD has Judged.” It is so stated (in II Chron. 17:1): [THEN <HIS SON> JEHOSHAPHAT REIGNED] <IN HIS STEAD,> AND HE STRENGTHENED HIMSELF OVER ISRAEL. What is the meaning of [AND HE STRENGTHENED HIMSELF (rt.: HZQ)]? That HE STRENGTHENED HIMSELF, when he appointed jud

CHAPTER XVII. Jehoshaphat prospereth—He sendeth Levites with the princes to teach Judah—His enemies terrified by God—His greatness. A™ Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his: stead, and strengthened himself against Israel.
43. And Joakim his son reigned in his stead: for when he was made king he was eighteen years
which [belonged] to the kings; for they said, He [is] a leper: and Jotham his son reigned in his stead.

3 And the Lord was with .Jehosh phat, because he walked in the first ways of his father David, and sought not unto Baalim;
and sought not unto Baalim --a term used for idols generally in contradistinction to the Lord God of his father. (Commentary by ROBERT JAMIESON)

Who were "his own" who "received him not"? Were they the Jews in general? Or were they the Qumran community? Jesus fulfilled all of the prophecies that were to be fulfilled by the Messiah except those which would be accomplished at his second coming. It is clear, however, that a great number of people rejected him for not having immediately fulfilled the others also. The main stumbling block to the Essenes would have been their unique reliance on the Aaronic Priesthood, knowing not that the…

Jesus fulfilled all of the prophecies that were to be fulfilled by the Messiah except those which would be accomplished at his second coming. It is clear, however, that a great number of people rejected him for not having immediately fulfilled the others also. The main stumbling block to the Essenes would have been their unique reliance on the Aaronic Priesthood, knowing not that the…

10 And the fear'of the Lord fell upon all the kingdoms of the lands that were round about Judah, so that they made no war against Jehoshaphat,
style of the sacred historian to trace this profound peace to the "fear of the Lord having fallen on all kingdoms of the lands that were round about Judah." (Commentary by ROBERT JAMIESON)

2. Now, in the third year of this reign, he called together the rulers
of the country, and the priests, and commanded them to go round the
land, and teach all the people that were under him, city by city, the
laws of Moses, and to keep them, and to be diligent in the worship of
God. With this the whole multitude was so pleased, that they were not so
eagerly set upon or affected with any thing so much as the observation
of the laws. The neighboring nations also continued to love Jehoshaphat,
and…
